title: asperula oppositifolia subsp. cabulica
intro: Asperula oppositifolia subsp. cabulica, also known as Afghan Woodruff or Mountain Woodruff, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, contributing to its health benefits, such as antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can help to protect against infections and oxidative stress. Asperula oppositifolia subsp. cabulica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, to be used for medicinal purposes.
